title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, and AWS SDK for JavaScript v3 · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A Node incident packet contains a plausible AWS SDK v3 TypeScript client contract with one unsafe assertion, stale object identity, unbounded stream, lost rejection, abort leak, cache defect, policy gap, or recovery overclaim; preserve the symptom and isolate first cause. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in AWS SDK v3 TypeScript client contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pra…
objectives: Pin Node 24.18.0, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 whose compiler reports 6.0.3, and @aws-sdk/client-s3, lib-storage, s3-request-presigner, and cloudfront-signer 3.1087.0 as one reviewed compatibility contract.
Construct S3Client from immutable process configuration, short-lived credential and region providers, one owned request handler, and explicit shutdown and test lifetimes.
Treat command inputs, optional output fields, enums, paginators, metadata, and streaming bodies as SDK transport shapes that still require runtime and business validation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Inspect the failure record · Debug Runtime Sdk Version
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8657 during a Node stream backpressure incident. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce pinned Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, modular client, runtime validation, and service-error narrowing; retain keyboard and screen-reader progress, cancellation, error, and recovery evidence; then abort during a stream or multipart promise and prove every task and part reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Node 24.18.0, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 whose compiler reports 6.0.3, and @aws-sdk/client-s3, lib-storage, s3-request-presigner, and cloudfront-signer 3.1087.0 as one reviewed compatibility contract.

Inspect the Runtime Sdk Version trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
